Clinical Notes — January 2024: Patient is a 42-year-old female presenting for follow-up of hypertension and chronic migraine. Blood pressure today is 138/88 mmHg, improved from 152/96 at the previous visit following initiation of amlodipine 5mg daily. Patient reports two migraine episodes in the past month, reduced from five per month prior to topiramate prophylaxis. Plan: continue current medications, add lifestyle counseling for sodium restriction, and recheck BP in six weeks.
